title: Background Research Response
description: Schema for background research output - finds authoritative sources and starting tables before column definition

tablewide_research.description: 2-3 paragraph summary of the domain, key facts, and context that will help with column definition. Should include: (1) How entities are typically identified/catalogued, (2) What authoritative sources exist, (3) What challenges might arise in discovery, (4) Any domain-specific considerations.

authoritative_sources.description: List of databases, directories, APIs, or lists that contain the target entities

description.description: What it contains and why it's valuable

coverage.description: How comprehensive (percentage, count, or qualitative assessment)

update_frequency.description: How often the source is updated

starting_tables.description: Explicit examples of existing tables/lists with actual entity names (NOT just URLs). Each table must include sample entities.

source_url.description: URL to the actual page with the list

entity_type.description: What type of entities (companies, people, papers, jobs, etc.)

sample_entities.description: CRITICAL: Actual sample entities from this source (NOT just URLs!). Must include identifying details, not just names. For COMPLETE ENUMERATION cases (paper references, countries, etc.), provide ALL entities here, not just a sample.

items.description: Entity with identifying details (e.g., 'Dr. Jane Smith - Stanford University - Neural Networks for Medical Imaging' OR '[1] Vaswani et al. - Attention Is All You Need - arXiv:1706.03762')

is_complete_enumeration.description: Set to true if this starting table represents a COMPLETE, EXHAUSTIVE list where ALL entities are provided (e.g., all references from a specific paper, all countries in Africa, all U.S. states). When true, sample_entities should contain ALL entities, not just a sample.

completeness.description: How complete/comprehensive this source is (percentage, count estimate, or qualitative)

update_frequency.description: How often this source is updated

discovery_notes.description: Optional notes on how to use this source for discovery

discovery_patterns.description: How entities are typically discovered in this domain

primary_pattern.description: Primary discovery pattern for this domain

description.description: Explanation of how entities are typically found

common_identifiers.description: How entities in this domain are typically identified

data_availability.description: Assessment of how easy/hard it will be to find and validate this data

identified_tables.description: Optional: Tables identified that have extractable structure and should be passed to table extraction (Step 0b). Only include if you found specific table URLs with clear structure.

url.description: URL where the table can be found

estimated_rows.description: Approximate number of rows in the table (can be number or string like '~50')

columns.description: Expected column names in the table

extract_table.description: Set to true to trigger table extraction (Step 0b) for this URL

target_rows.description: Optional: Filter or specification for which rows to extract (e.g., 'only winners', 'School Committee only', 'top 10 ranked'). If omitted, extract all rows from the table.

extraction_priority.description: Priority for extraction. High = user specifically requested, medium = good source, low = opportunistic

notes.description: Optional notes about this table (structure, pagination, access requirements)
